title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to establish qualifications for the appointment of a person as a marriage and family therapist, qualified to provide clinical supervision, in the Veterans Health Administration.
stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill sets new rules for marriage and family therapists who want to supervise other therapists at Veterans Affairs hospitals. To get this job, they must have a master's degree and be licensed in their state. They also must be allowed by their state to supervise other therapists, or be approved as a supervisor by a national marriage and family therapy group. These new requirements will help make sure veterans get good care from well-trained therapists.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Establishment of Qualifications for the Appointment of a Person as a Marriage and Family Therapist, Qualified to Provide Clinical Supervision, in the Veterans Health Administration
sections.text: This section changes the rules about who can be hired as a marriage and family therapist at Veterans Affairs hospitals and clinics. The changes are made to section seven thousand four hundred two of title thirty eight of the United States Code. The law currently has requirements for marriage and family therapists. This bill adds new rules for therapists who want to supervise other therapists. To get this type of job, a person must meet all the regular requirements for marriage and family therapists. They must also be approved by their state government to provide clinical supervision. Clinical supervision means watching over and training other therapists who are still learning their job.
